channel.title: Taiwanese American Historical Society
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Taiwanese American Historical Society

item.title: Taiwan Minpao (臺灣民報)
item.description: Taiwan Minpao (臺灣民報) Overview The Taiwan Minpao (臺灣民報, “Taiwan People’s Newspaper”) was a Taiwanese-language newspaper founded in 1923 during the Japanese colonial period (1895-1945).

item.title: Community Federation (社團聯合會)
item.description: Community Federation (社團聯合會) Overview The Community Federation (社團聯合會) was an umbrella organization for Taiwanese American community groups in the Los Angeles area during the 1980s.

item.title: CAHR (Chinese Association for Human Rights)
item.description: CAHR (Chinese Association for Human Rights) Overview Founded by: Hsu-Wen-pin-許文彬 (Hsu Wen-Pin) CAHR (Chinese Association for Human Rights) was an advocacy organization founded by Hsu-Wen-pin-許文彬 to advance human rights causes, particularly focusing on indigenous communities in Taiwan.

item.title: NATPA (North America Taiwanese Professors' Association)
item.description: NATPA (North America Taiwanese Professors’ Association) Overview The North America Taiwanese Professors’ Association (NATPA) is a professional and academic organization for Taiwanese professors and scholars in North America.

item.title: Lai Ho Cultural and Educational Foundation (賴和文教基金會)
item.description: Lai Ho Cultural and Educational Foundation (賴和文教基金會) Overview Founder: Lai-Yuehyan-賴悅顏 (Lai Yueh-Yan) The Lai Ho Cultural and Educational Foundation was established by 賴悅顏 (Lai Yueh-Yan), grandson of Lai-Ho-賴和 (Lai Ho, 1894-1943), to preserve and promote his grandfather’s literary legacy and Taiwane...

item.title: LA Taiwanese Cultural Association (大洛杉磯台灣會館)
item.description: LA Taiwanese Cultural Association (大洛杉磯台灣會館) Overview The LA Taiwanese Cultural Association (大洛杉磯台灣會館) is a community center and cultural venue in the San Gabriel Valley area of Los Angeles, serving as a gathering place for Taiwanese Americans in Southern California.

item.title: Evergreen Fellowship (長青團契)
item.description: Evergreen Fellowship (長青團契) Overview The Evergreen Fellowship (長青團契) was an elder fellowship group within the Formosan-Presbyterian-Church-Los-Angeles-FPCLA (Formosa Presbyterian Church, Los Angeles).

item.title: 賴悅顏 (Lai Yueh-Yan)
item.description: 賴悅顏 Lai Yueh-Yan Biographical Information Names: 賴悅顏 (Hanzi) / Lai Yueh-Yan (Wade-Giles) Role in the Community 賴悅顏 is the grandson of Lai-Ho-賴和 (Lai Ho, 1894-1943), known as the “Father of Taiwanese New Literature.” He founded the Lai-Ho-Cultural-and-Educational-Foundation to preserve and promote hi...

item.title: 何汝諧 (Ho Yuh-Hsia)
item.description: 何汝諧 Ho Yuh-Hsia Biographical Information Names: 何汝諧 (Hanzi) / Ho Yuh-Hsia (Wade-Giles) Role in the Community 何汝諧 was the first president of the NATPA-South-California-Chapter (North America Taiwanese Professors’ Association, Southern California Chapter).

item.title: 王春雄 (Wang Chunxiong)
item.description: 王春雄 Wang Chunxiong Biographical Information Names: 王春雄 (Hanzi) / Wang Chunxiong (Pinyin) Role in the Community 王春雄 was a founding supporter of the Formosan-Presbyterian-Church-Los-Angeles-FPCLA (Formosa Presbyterian Church, Los Angeles).
